notes: MB embedded_mbid authoritative: title/date 1958/Verve all match; artist credit includes Roy Eldridge, Sonny Stitt, Jo Jones — exactly as DNA states. All keyTracks (Will You Still Be Mine?, Joy Spring, Gal In Calico, 52nd Street Theme, Monitor Blues) present in MB tracklist. DNA tracks Joy Spring/52nd Street Theme/Monitor Blues all confirmed. NOTE: evidence.wikipedia.storedUrl (Oscar_Peterson_and_Roy_Eldridge) is a DIFFERENT 1975 album, but ours.wikipediaUrl is null so nothing wrong is shipped.

MB embedded_mbid (Count Basie, Pablo) and Wikipedia 'Kansas City 8: Get Together' (1979 studio album by Count Basie) both match. Wiki personnel lists 8 players, supporting DNA's 'octet'. All keyTracks match MB tracklist. DNA tracks Ode to Pres / Basie's Bag confirmed. Stored wiki URL is the correct entity.

MB returned no release. Wikipedia 'Pimp Master' (2005 studio album by Soil & "Pimp" Sessions) matches entity. Wiki confirms: second studio album, Japanese group, released June 23 2005, six performing members (Shacho, Motoharu sax, Tabu Zombie trumpet, Josei piano, Akita Goldman bass, Midorin drums) supporting DNA's 'sextet'; Victor credited in personnel supporting label=Victor. 'death jazz'/'most explosive'/'reckless abandon' are editorial. Wiki has no track listing so keyTracks (My Foolish Heart, Waltz for Debby, Pimp Master, Rumba Azul) are no_evidence — not contradicted.

MB embedded_mbid authoritative: title 'The Man I Love', date 2002-04-09, artist Billie Holiday, label Dreyfus Jazz, secondaryType Compilation — all match ours. DNA tracks 'God Bless the Child' and 'Lover Man' both in MB tracklist. All 6 keyTracks present in MB tracklist (including the source-preserved typo 'As Yime Goes By', which matches MB verbatim). 'big band era' framing is contextual/editorial for a compilation of older recordings, not a contradicted claim. NOTE: evidence.wikipedia.storedUrl ('The Man I Love (album)' = 1957 Peggy Lee album) is a WRONG entity, but ours.wikipediaUrl is null so nothing wrong is shipped.

websearch resolved=true (Nonesuch, freejazzblog, Discogs). Repaired DNA fully matches the resolved findings/note: Mary Halvorson's Nonesuch debut, two paired suites (2022); Amaryllis = six-part sextet suite (guitar, vibraphone, trombone, trumpet, bass, drums) with Mivos Quartet on three of six tracks; Belladonna = five through-composed pieces for solo guitar + the same string quartet; produced by John Dieterich. year=2022 and label='Nonesuch' confirmed. keyTracks: Amaryllis, Night Shift, Moonburn all confirmed against the (Amaryllis-side) findings tracklist [Night Shift, Anesthesia, Amaryllis, Side Effect, Hoodwink, 892 Teeth, Moonburn]; 'Belladonna' could not be confirmed because the Belladonna-suite tracklist was not enumerated in evidence (only Moonburn named as its 2nd track) — not contradicted, just unenumerated. The prior-state bad keyTrack 'The Gate' flagged in the websearch note is no longer present in ours.keyTracks.

MB embedded_mbid: 'Les Genies du Jazz... Fletcher "Smack" Henderson', artist Fletcher Henderson, Compilation — matches entity. DNA's named sides (Sugar Foot Stomp, The Stampede, Henderson Stomp) all in MB tracklist; all 5 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. year=null is not contradicted (MB date empty). NOTE: wikipedia opensearch resolved to 'Smak (album)' (Serbian band) — wrong entity, discarded; ours.wikipediaUrl is null.

MB search (topArtistSimilarity 1.0) Glenn Miller compilation 'Moonlight Serenade' matches. All DNA-named tracks (In the Mood, Chattanooga Choo Choo, Pennsylvania 6-5000, Tuxedo Junction, American Patrol) and all 6 keyTracks present in MB tracklist. DNA's '1939 theme' confirmed by wiki disambiguation ('a 1939 American popular song with original music by Glenn Miller'). FIELD NOTE (not flagged as error): ours.year=1992 / label='Various' vs the single matched MB edition (2001, Rajon Music Group, NZ) — these are different pressings of a much-reissued compilation, so the matched edition does not contradict a separate 1992 edition; 'Various' is a comp-source placeholder, not a false specific label claim.

MB embedded_mbid: title 'Now's The Time', date 2020 (matches ours.year=2020), artist Charlie Parker. DNA's named tracks (The Song Is You, Laird Baird, Kim, Chi Chi, Cosmic Rays) all in MB tracklist; all 6 keyTracks present. DNA '1945 composition' confirmed by wiki ('first recording... on November 26, 1945'). DNA framed as 'a reissue gathering... recordings'. FIELD NOTE (not flagged as error): ours.label='Various' vs MB authoritative label 'Bird's Nest'; 'Various' is a compilation-source placeholder (Parker originals span Savoy/Dial/Clef/Mercury), not a contradicted specific claim — left to repair-writer's discretion.

MB search returned 'Afro-Paris / I Cover the Waterfront' — a DIFFERENT release (1953 single), not our album; discarded for fact-checking. Wikipedia 'Afro (album)' (1954 studio album by Dizzy Gillespie) matches entity and confirms ours.year=1954 ('released in November 1954') and label 'Norgran Records'. albumDNA is generic editorial template (no specific false claims). All 6 keyTracks match wiki tracklist (Manteca Theme, Contraste, Jungla, Rhumba Finale, A Night in Tunisia, Con Alma).

MB search (1.0) and Wikipedia both match: Tadd Dameron, Prestige (P-7037), 1956. Wiki confirms verbatim every specific DNA claim: 'Recorded March 9, 1956', title track 'a through-composed composition with no solos', and "'Flossie Lou' is a contrafact of 'Jeepers Creepers'". All 5 keyTracks match the full MB/wiki tracklist.

websearch resolved=true (Wikipedia We Insist!). Most DNA claims confirmed by the evidence note: drummer Max Roach, subtitle 'Max Roach's Freedom Now Suite', recorded 1960, released 1961 on Candid, produced by Nat Hentoff, Abbey Lincoln vocals; ours.year=1961 and label=Candid now correct (prior bad 2011/Various repaired). All 5 keyTracks match the findings tracklist (the prior 6th track 'Oh Yeah, Oh Yeah' flagged in the note is no longer present). HOWEVER one substantive DNA claim — 'added to the U.S. National Recording Registry in 2022' — is NOT covered by the provided evidence (note/findings do not mention it). Not contradicted, but uncheckable against provided evidence; classified unverifiable per spec. A repair-writer/human should confirm or remove the Registry claim from primary sources.

MB search and Wikipedia 'Night Train (Oscar Peterson album)' both confirm 1963, Verve, Oscar Peterson Trio — matches ours.year=1963. albumDNA is generic editorial template (no specific false claims). All 6 keyTracks match MB tracklist (C-Jam Blues = MB 'C Jam Blues').

Treated as ok.

Entity certain — MB search (1.0): title 'Inside Hi-Fi', Lee Konitz, Atlantic (1258); all 6 keyTracks match MB tracklist. Discrepancy: ours.year=1957, but MB gives 1956 in BOTH release date and releaseGroup.firstReleaseDate, and the albumDNA itself says 'Recorded in 1956'. The field year=1957 contradicts the only year evidence available (and the prose). Flagged as factual_error on the year field; confidence moderate because some discographies place the release at 1957 vs 1956 recording — a human should reconcile to MB's 1956 or a sourced 1957 release date.

MB search (1.0) and Wikipedia both match: Wes Montgomery, Riverside, 1962, Live. Wiki confirms every specific DNA claim verbatim: 'recorded live at Tsubo in Berkeley, California, on June 25, 1962', 'quintet that included Wynton Kelly on piano, Johnny Griffin on tenor saxophone, Paul Chambers on bass, and Jimmy Cobb on drums'. All 6 keyTracks match (take-suffixed versions match the 2007 reissue tracklist).

MB search (1.0): title 'Plenty, Plenty Soul', 1957, Milt Jackson, Atlantic (1269) — matches ours.year=1957. albumDNA is generic editorial template; the 'Cool Jazz' genre tag is editorial opinion, not flagged. All 6 keyTracks match MB tracklist.

Wikipedia 'Undercurrent (Bill Evans and Jim Hall album)' (1962) matches entity and confirms every specific DNA claim: '1962 jazz album by the pianist Bill Evans and the guitarist Jim Hall', 'recorded in the spring of 1962', opener 'My Funny Valentine' as 'the single up-tempo romp', Hall's 'Romain', and John Lewis's 'Skating in Central Park'. All 6 keyTracks match MB tracklist. FIELD NOTE (not flagged): ours.label='Various' vs the matched MB compilation edition (2013, Not Now Music); original issue was United Artists with many reissues, so 'Various' is a comp placeholder rather than a contradicted specific label.
